channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Progressive Is Biggest Auto Insurer, Surpassing State Farm: S&P GMI
item.description: The numbers aren’t all in, but it looks like Progressive is now the biggest private passenger auto insurer, based on an analysis from S&P Global Market Intelligence that shows Progressive muscling past State Farm. Making the adjustment produces a history-making result—ending State Farm’s 84-year reign as the nation’s top private auto insurer, S&P GMI said, noting that State Farm has ranked No. Even with no adjustments, Progressive’s first-quarter 2026 private auto direct premiums written of $18.1 billion surpassed State Farm’s $17.1 billion, marking the first time Progressive exceeded State Farm in a single quarter, S&P GMI noted.

item.title: No pain, no gain: who will be left once the Indian dust settles?
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Liberty Mutual hikes stake in India unit to 74%
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FAA Admits Faulure to Act on Warnings Before Jet-Copter Collision That Killed 67 People
item.description: The head of the Federal Aviation Administration will tell Congress on Tuesday the agency failed to act on warnings prior to the January 2025 fatal collision between an American Airlines regional jet and an Army helicopter that killed 67 people near Reagan Washington National Airport. In January, the National Transportation Safety Board said a series of systemic failures by the Federal Aviation Administration led to a devastating mid-air collision that was the deadliest U.S. He said there is an ongoing strategic sweeping reorganization of the FAA “that includes streamlining leadership roles (and) eliminating silos which hinder transparency and information sharing.” The NTSB determined the collision was caused by the FAA’s decision to allow helicopters to travel close to the airport without safeguards to separate them from airplanes, and its failure to review data and act on recommendations to move helicopter traffic away from the airport.

item.title: Core Travel Launches Group Travel Insurance Platform
item.description: Core Travel Insurance (New Lebanon, N.Y.) has launched a digital platform built by Openkoda (Wrocław, Poland) to support group travel medical insurance for organizations, brokers and insured travelers. “Launching this platform is a key step in transforming the way we serve organizations and travelers,” comments Fiona Lally, CEO, Core Travel Insurance. “We are proud to support Core Travel Insurance in launching a platform that combines speed, flexibility, and customer-focused innovation,” comments Arkadiusz Drysch, CEO, Openkoda.

item.title: Sixfold Offers AI Underwriting Through Microsoft Marketplace
item.description: Sixfold (New York) has made its AI underwriting solution available through Microsoft Marketplace from Microsoft (Redmond, Wash.), giving insurers a new channel for discovering, procuring and deploying the company’s technology. “By integrating with the Microsoft Marketplace, we’re making Sixfold and our underwriting AI brain easier to discover and deploy,” comments Roger Ferrandis, Head of Partnerships, Sixfold. Availability through Microsoft Marketplace is intended to let insurers align Sixfold adoption with existing cloud budgets and infrastructure, reducing approval cycles and deployment barriers.

item.title: 34,000 PG&E Customers Without Power on California Fire Risk
item.description: More than 34,000 homes and businesses remain without power across PG&E Corp.’s service area after a weekend outage due in part to dry and windy conditions that raised the risk of wild fires. Related: California Utility Bills Are 20% Higher Due to Wildfires The electricity outages follow an earlier warning from PG&E of possible so-called proactive power cuts—which can range from 18 hours to several days—due to heightened fire threats. Dry wind gusts of up to 50 miles (80 kilometers) per hour risk toppling power lines and other equipment that could spark blazes.

item.title: Musk Expects Widespread Use of Cars Without Human Monitors in U.S. This Year
item.description: Tesla chief Elon Musk said on Monday he expects fully self-driving cars without human safety monitors to become more widespread in the United States later this year, after being introduced in Texas. Speaking via a video link to the Smart Mobility Summit in Tel Aviv, Musk said self-driving cars operate in Texas without safety monitors, and that would expand nationwide this year. Yet, Musk – who has made bold predictions on autonomous vehicles for over a decade, many of which have not materialized on his timelines – remains upbeat that cars with no humans will be ubiquitous within a decade.

item.title: Despite Dip in Cargo Theft in Q1 This Year, Sharp Increase in Fraud Schemes Seen
item.description: Despite a year-over-year decline in cargo theft in the first quarter of 2026, a first in five years, fraud schemes surged, according to a new report from in-transit supply chain risk management platform Overhaul. Despite the reduced rate, fraud-based schemes grew, and new product categories emerged as targets, while thefts spread into regions that hadn’t been hotspots in the past. Q1-2026 Cargo Theft Report recorded 574 incidents across the U.S., an average of 6.4 per day.

item.title: Solid underwriting results continued in 2025 for Leadenhall backed Nectaris Re: Am Best
item.description: Nectaris Re Ltd., the Class 3A Bermuda-based rated reinsurance platform backed by specialist insurance linked securities (ILS) and reinsurance investment manager Leadenhall Capital Partners LLP, continued to report solid underwriting results for a third consecutive year in 2025, according to a rating affirmation from AM Best. The rating agency said that it assesses the overall operating performance of Nectaris Re as adequate, based on “solid gross and net underwriting results from 2023 through 2025, along with the historical operating results of the reinsurance portfolio of the Leadenhall-managed funds from which the Nectaris Re portfolio was formed, and the projected performance results of the retained tail risk.” That indicates a third consecutive year of strong underwriting profits for Nectaris Re, with that performance reflective of how the subject reinsurance business backed by ILS funds under the management of Leadenhall Capital Partners will have delivered returns for investors in those strategies, we assume. Nectaris Re continued to benefit from the higher interest rate environment in 2025, which has again served to deliver additional investment income, even though its focus remains on the underwriting profit side of the business.

item.title: Augment Risk appoints Crystal Maragh to Global Parametric Specialty team
item.description: Augment Risk, a reinsurance and capital solutions broker, has confirmed that Crystal Maragh has joined its Global Parametric Specialty division in Miami. Kurt Cripps, Partner and Global Head of Parametric Specialty at Augment Risk, commented: “We are delighted to welcome Crystal to the Augment Risk team. Maragh arrives at Augment Risk from NormanMax Insurance Holdings, where she was Vice President of pricing.

item.title: US P&C insurance industry faces weaker growth in 2026 despite improved underwriting results
item.description: The US property and casualty (P&C) insurance industry is projected to see underlying growth fall to -3.7% in the first half of 2026, down from 1.6% in 2025, as insurers continue to contend with catastrophe exposure, inflationary pressures and rising claims costs, according to new forecasts from the Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I) and actuarial and consulting firm Milliman. Despite the more difficult outlook for 2026, the industry recorded stronger underwriting performance in 2025. The organisations also reported improved homeowners insurance results despite ongoing catastrophe activity, including the Los Angeles wildfires earlier in the year.

item.title: Dominick Hoare to retire at year-end after more than 32 years with Munich Re
item.description: Dominick Hoare, Group Chief Underwriting Officer, Munich Re Specialty, Global Markets and Active Underwriting Syndicate 457, is set to retire at the end of 2026 after more than 32 years of service. John Wilkinson, CEO of Munich Re Specialty, Global Markets, said: “Dominick has made an exceptional contribution to Munich Re over more than three decades. Hoare joined Munich Re, one of Europe’s big four reinsurance companies, in 1994 and has held various senior underwriting and leadership positions during his tenure.

item.title: Inszone Acquires Florida’s Coastal Insurance, Shepherd Moves to New Bradenton Office
item.description: Also in Florida, Shepherd Insurance announced it is moving its office from Sarasota to Pendery Place in the Bradenton/Lakewood Ranch area. California-headquartered Inszone Insurance Services has continued its hot streak of acquisitions with the purchase of Coastal Insurance Services in Florida and affiliated Optimal Insurance Solutions. The Sarasota office saw the coming together of five agencies over the past 13 years through acquisitions, making the Bradenton move a natural next step in the firm’s long-term growth plan for the Florida market, Shepherd leadership said.

item.title: Business Moves: Vienna Insurance Purchases Nuernberger; Liberty Mutual Raises Stake in India Unit; Aon’s NFP Buys Irish Private Wealth Specialist; Gallagher Buys UK Law Firm
item.description: Liberty Mutual Raises Stake in India Unit to 74% Vienna Insurance Group Completes Purchase of Germany’s Nuernberger Vienna Insurance Group (VIG) has completed its acquisition of Nuernberger Beteiligungs AG for 1.38 billion euros ($1.6 billion), in VIG’s largest-ever transaction. “As part of Vienna Insurance Group, we will significantly accelerate our transformation into a prevention insurer — a strong positioning from which our customers, our sales partners and our employees will benefit,” according to Harald Rosenberger, CEO of Nuernberger.

item.title: Michigan Secures $108M Settlement with Monsanto for PCB Contamination
item.description: Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel has secured a $108 million settlement with Monsanto Company (PDF) , the major manufacturer of polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs), and affiliated companies Solutia Inc. and Pharmacia LLC to address harms from PCB contamination in the State of Michigan. The Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE) and the Michigan Department of Natural Resources (DNR) will jointly oversee the use of funds to remediate PCB-contaminated property and resources and restore natural resources adversely impacted by contamination.

item.title: DEMO: Your Product Name
item.description: We recommend limiting this to 1 to 2 paragraphs, so the reader can quickly get to watching your Demo Video. Are you interested in learning more about and about this product? (You may select any or all of these bullet points.

item.title: AI vs. Automation: The Shift That’s Changing Agency Workflows
item.description: Automation: The Shift That’s Changing Agency Workflows appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Automation: Rules-based execution within your workflows Automation is a rule-based execution. Well-designed automation ensures consistency, reduces manual effort, and keeps workflows moving.

item.title: Insurance Data and the Complex Loops Redefining Casualty Risk
item.description: Our understanding of risk, and the insurance data we rely on to quantify it, is increasingly misaligned with how people live, work and transact today. The world is evolving at a pace that outstrips traditional casualty models. This disconnect is creating challenges for insurers as they navigate a rapidly shifting landscape of liability and exposure.

item.title: Snap, YouTube, TikTok Settle School Suit Targeting Social Media
item.description: Snap Inc., Google’s YouTube and ByteDance Ltd.’s TikTok reached agreements to settle the first lawsuit headed to trial over claims that addiction to top social media platforms has disrupted learning and pushed public schools to spend massive sums fighting a mental health crisis, according to court filings. The trial will serve as a test case for more than 1,200 similar lawsuits nationwide in which school districts allege that the biggest social media companies have harmed students so badly that it’s undermining the education system. “This matter has been amicably resolved and our focus remains on building age-appropriate products and parental controls that deliver on that promise.” In a separate statement, a Snap spokesperson said the parties “are pleased to have been able to resolve this matter in an amicable manner.” A group of lawyers representing school districts said in a statement that “our focus remains on pursuing justice for the remaining 1,200 school districts who have filed cases.” TikTok didn’t immediately respond to a request for comment.

item.title: Industry Concerned as Supreme Court Revives Lawsuit Against Freight Broker
item.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday allowed a man to sue a major logistics company after he lost part of his leg in a semi tractor-trailer crash, a decision that could have big ripple effects across the trucking industry. Robinson, the country’s largest freight broker by size, over its role in putting the driver on the road despite what he called “serious red flags.” The decision does not mean Montgomery will necessarily win the lawsuit, which the company is contesting. But the ruling opens the door to increased liability for freight brokers, a key part of the industry.

item.title: Our response to the Treasury’s policy statement on Consumer Credit Act reform
item.description: The Treasury has published its policy statement today on reform of the Consumer Credit Act 1974 (CCA). We will continue to work closely with the Treasury, Government, Parliament, consumer bodies and other stakeholders as the reform programme develops. Reform of the CCA is an important step towards a more flexible regime that supports effective competition and innovation, while maintaining appropriate consumer protection both now and in the future.

item.title: Sector Snapshot: Quantum Computing Startup Investment Slows In 2026 While Public Markets Hold Strong
item.description: These are pretty good times to be a public quantum computing company. Around the same time, Quantum Motion , a London-based startup focused on silicon transistor-based quantum computing, announced a $160 million Series C co-led by DCVC and Kembara . The numbers: So far this year, companies in Crunchbase’s quantum computing category have pulled in $1.2 billion in seed- through growth-stage funding.

item.title: NJM Insurance returns for third cat bond, targets $150m+ Lower Ferry Re 2026-1
item.description: NJM Insurance has returned to the catastrophe bond market to sponsor its third transaction, initially seeking $150 million or more in northeast US named storm reinsurance from the capital markets with this Lower Ferry Re Ltd. This Lower Ferry Re Series 2026-1 cat bond offering will provide NJM Insurance with a source of multi-year US named storm reinsurance protection across the Northeast US states of New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Delaware, New York, Connecticut, Maryland and Ohio, the same states as the maturing 2023 deal. The reinsurance protection from this Lower Ferry Re 2026-1 cat bond will cover NJM Insurance across a three-year term, to the end of June 2029, on an indemnity and per-occurrence basis, we are told.

item.title: AmCoastal lifts first-event reinsurance limit to $1.68bn at mid-year 2026 renewal
item.description: American Coastal Insurance Company (AmCoastal) has successfully renewed its core catastrophe reinsurance program for 2026/27, with first event limit now extending to $1.68 billion, as the company secured 14.4% more in coverage for $1.918 billion of occurrence-based limit in the aggregate. During the company’s recent earnings call for Q1 2026, CEO Brad Martz, whilst speaking about AmCoastal’s mid-year 2026 reinsurance renewal, explained that reinsurance costs are moving in the right direction for the company, which helped it to buy a larger reinsurance tower this year. Retentions have risen, but the company has continued to use its own reinsurance captive to assist with that, with a first-event retention of $49 million, with $26.5 million retained by AmCoastal and $22.5 million retained by its captive, an increase of $19.25 million from the $29.75 million retention in force a year ago.

item.title: City of Zurich Pension ILS investments grow to US $1.58bn, returned 6.9% in 2025
item.description: The City of Zurich Pension Fund saw its investments in insurance-linked securities (ILS) strategies delivering a positive return of 6.9% in 2025, while the value of the ILS allocation rose meaningfully in the last year, to around US $1.58 billion by April 30th 2026. When we last reported on the City of Zurich Pension Fund (or Pensionskasse Stadt Zürich) and its investments into the insurance-linked securities sector just over one year ago, the pension had benefited from a 9.2% return in 2024, while the value of its investments in USD had reached just over $1.15 billion by March 2025 . Across all of its asset class investments, the City of Zurich Pension reported a 7.2% return for 2025, with ILS delivering a 6.9% return and valuable diversification for its strategy.

item.title: CFC appoints John Howard as Chair of its Board of Directors
item.description: CFC, a specialist insurance provider centred on emerging risks and recognised as a market leader in cyber insurance, has appointed industry veteran John Howard as Chair, subject to regulatory approval. As I step into the role of Senior Independent Non-Executive Director, I look forward to supporting the Board and ensuring a smooth and confident transition.” Howard added, “I would like to take this opportunity to thank Richard, who has provided the business with outstanding leadership, support and constructive challenge throughout his tenure as Chair. Richard Ward, who has served as Chair since 2022, will continue to serve on CFC’s board as Senior Independent Non-Executive Director.

item.title: Premium renewal rates up YoY across nearly all major commercial lines: Ivans
item.description: Ivans, a division of Applied Systems, has released the April 2026 results of the Ivans Index, indicating a year over year increase in average premium renewal rates across Commercial Auto, Business Owner’s Policy (BOP), General Liability, Commercial Property, and Umbrella. The monthly Ivans Index reports data-driven trends in premium rate renewal change for the most placed commercial insurance lines. Conversely, Workers’ Compensation experienced a decrease in premium renewal rates during the same period.

item.title: CopperPoint to acquire General Indemnity Group in nationwide surety expansion
item.description: CopperPoint Insurance Company, a Phoenix-based specialty provider of workers’ compensation and commercial insurance solutions, has signed a definitive agreement to acquire General Indemnity Group (GIG), an insurance holding company focused on commercial and contract surety solutions across all 50 states and Washington D.C. The deal represents CopperPoint’s third acquisition in the past decade and expands the company’s capabilities in commercial and contract surety, while strengthening its national presence and distribution network. “We look forward to partnering with the entire GIG team to build together on their decade-long track record of growth and innovation, fuelled by the same kinds of investments in people, partnerships, and platforms that CopperPoint has long prioritised.” Founded in 2015, GIG has developed a national footprint in the surety sector, supporting tens of thousands of bonds linked to construction, commercial, and public sector projects.

item.title: Half of Pilots Killed in US Accidents Tested Positive for Drugs
item.description: More than half of pilots killed in US civil aviation accidents between 2018 and 2022 tested positive for at least one drug, the US National Transportation Safety Board said in a report published May 14. One of the biggest increases was in the illicit drugs category, which rose to 7.4% of cases, driven by more pilots testing positive for the primary psychoactive chemical found in marijuana. Of the 984pilots who died in crashes and other fatal events, tests in about 29% of cases detected “potentially impairing drugs” that could diminish a pilot’s performance, the report said.

item.title: Miami Condo Developer Kapoor Changes Plea to Guilty in $89 Million Fraud Case
item.description: A prominent Miami real estate developer who once hired the city’s mayor as a $10,000-a-month consultant has pleaded guilty to money laundering and payroll-tax charges in a $89 million fraud scheme. Rishi Kapoor, who once led Location Ventures and URBIN development firms, now faces more than 10 years in federal prison and $70 million in restitution to former investors and the Internal Revenue Service, according to court documents and local news reports. Kapoor was accused of raising millions of dollars from investors for major condominium and housing developments in the Miami area, then failing to build the projects and diverting much of the money for his personal use, Kapoor’s March 3 indictment shows.

item.title: CopperPoint Insurance to Acquire Surety Specialist General Indemnity Group
item.description: Boston-based General Indemnity Group (GIG), an insurance holding company specializing in commercial and contract surety, announced that it has agreed to be acquired by CopperPoint Insurance Co., a workers’ compensation and commercial insurance provider based in Phoenix. The transaction includes GIG’s subsidiaries United Casualty & Surety Insurance Co. The deal gives CopperPoint nationwide surety capabilities and a countrywide platform.

item.title: Input 1 embeds billing and payments into Insuresoft platform for VGM modernization
item.description: The post Input 1 embeds billing and payments into Insuresoft platform for VGM modernization appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Input 1 has completed the integration of its billing and digital payment infrastructure into the Diamond System from Insuresoft, supporting VGM Insurance Services as it modernizes its policy administration environment. VGM has used Input 1’s billing infrastructure for several years, and the migration involved coordinated work between teams from VGM, Insuresoft and Input 1 to maintain revenue operations during the platform transition.

item.title: Prudential to acquire 75% stake in Bharti Life Insurance
item.description: Prudential plc has agreed to acquire a 75% stake in Bharti Life Insurance Company Limited from Bharti Life Ventures and 360 ONE Asset Management for ₹3,500 crore (approximately $389 million), marking a significant expansion of its presence in India’s life insurance market. Regulatory requirements are expected to require the insurer to reduce its stake in ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Limited below 10%, prompting discussions with regulators regarding the timing and structure of any divestment. Following completion, Prudential’s India portfolio will include majority ownership of Bharti Life and Prudential HCL Health Insurance Limited, alongside minority positions in ICICI Prudential Asset Management and ICICI Prudential Life.

item.title: Aviva Appoints Alex McMurrough as Chief Underwriting Officer for Commercial Lines
item.description: Since joining Aviva last year, she has made a significant impact, recognised for her sharp insights, collaboration and results.” The post Aviva Appoints Alex McMurrough as Chief Underwriting Officer for Commercial Lines appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Insurance giant Aviva has elevated Alex McMurrough to one of its top underwriting positions, naming her chief underwriting officer for its commercial lines division. Her expanded responsibilities will see her take charge of underwriting strategy and risk appetite across Aviva’s commercial lines portfolio, with her remit now also stretching into technical underwriting.

item.title: CopperPoint to acquire General Indemnity Group
item.description: CopperPoint Insurance Company signed a definitive agreement to acquire General Indemnity Group and its subsidiaries from Boston Omaha Corporation . The deal will expand CopperPoint’s specialty offerings and establish countrywide operations. CopperPoint said the transaction is expected to close before year-end, subject to regulatory approvals.

item.title: Distinguished Programs opens marine cargo program
item.description: Distinguished Programs, a national MGA and insurance program manager, announced that its marine cargo program is officially open for business. Allianz’s backing provides a strong and stable foundation behind the program.” – Ralph Salce, president of Distinguished Marine Cargo. The new program further broadens Distinguished’s portfolio of specialty offerings including environmental, fine art and collectibles, real estate, hospitality, restaurant, transactional liability, community association, executive lines, surety, and inland marine.

item.title: Lemonade acquires Wilco
item.description: In a LinkedIn post, Lemonade co-founder Shai Wininger confirmed the acquisition of Wilco, a Tel Aviv-based startup offering an upskilling platform for software developers. Founded in 2021, Wilco publicly disclosed ~$10 million in funding. “Today, we’re announcing that we’re joining Lemonade.

item.title: Ruiz Re entra en País Vasco con la integración de Abando 2000
item.description: Ruiz Re ha iniciado su expansión en País Vasco con la integración de la correduría bilbaína Abando 2000, especializada tanto en seguros como en asesoramiento patrimonial y financiero. Según explica Juan David Ruiz, el grupo prevé anunciar nuevas incorporaciones en País Vasco en las próximas semanas y celebrará en Bilbao su convención nacional de 2027 como muestra de su apuesta por el norte. Además, Abando 2000 cuenta con una actividad destacada en el ámbito financiero, con 5 millones de euros en fondos de inversión y 5,5 millones en planes de pensiones, lo que eleva el patrimonio total gestionado hasta los 10,5 millones de euros .

item.title: Los planes de pensiones individuales alcanzan el 11,3% de rentabilidad anual en abril
item.description: La rentabilidad interanual para el conjunto de planes de pensiones del Sistema Individual se situó en abril en el 11,3% , según datos facilitados por Inverco, que destaca el comportamiento de los productos de Renta Variable, que alcanzan una revalorización del 26,3% a 1 año, 15,7% a 3 años y a 5 años un 10,1% . A más largo plazo ( 20 años ), los planes registraron una rentabilidad media anual neta del 3,1% . Netas de gastos y comisiones En abril, el volumen de activos del Sistema Individual se situó en 98.283 millones de euros, tras experimentar un incremento de 3.543 millones de euros debido a las revalorizaciones de los mercados financieros.

item.title: Sigue el movimiento de ahorradores hacia productos con más potencial de rentabilidad
item.description: El mayor dinamismo en captación de nuevos asegurados corresponde a los productos con mayor exposición a los mercados financieros. En el extremo opuesto, los productos que pierden cartera son precisamente aquellos cuyo ahorro gestionado también retrocede. El sector asegurador cerró el primer trimestre con 31.277.368 asegurados en seguros de vida —un 3,06% más que un año antes— y 5.524.648 partícipes en planes de pensiones gestionados por aseguradoras , que crecieron a un ritmo aún mayor, del 7,38% interanual.

item.title: Snap, YouTube Settle School-Social Media Suit Ahead of Trial
item.description: TikTok and Snap previously settled a first-of-its-kind personal injury suit over social media addiction shortly before it went to trial in Los Angeles earlier this year. reached agreements to settle the first lawsuit headed to trial over claims that addiction to top social media platforms has disrupted learning and pushed public schools to spend massive sums fighting a mental health crisis, according to court filings. Related: Meta Asks California Judge to Throw out Landmark Social Media Addiction Verdict The trial will serve as a test case for more than 1,200 similar lawsuits nationwide in which school districts allege that the biggest social media companies have harmed students so badly that it’s undermining the education system.

item.title: US Sees A 27% Increase in Recalled Products in Q1, Report Shows
item.description: recall report shows the volume of defective products recalled across five U.S. According to the report, the consumer products sector was the only industry to record an increase in recalls events, rising 14.5%. The automotive sector had its highest quarterly total in two years with 12.19 million units recalled, while the pharmaceutical sector reached a four-year high with 218.83 million units impacted, the report shows.

item.title: Steadfast-owned Miramar acquires Australian sports specialist MGA
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Kiwi broker Rothbury appoints Aon veteran Charlene Kitching as head of broking services
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Guy Carpenter names Andrew Hare as head of growth for APAC
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: FCA and Bank of England set out shared vision for tokenisation in UK wholesale markets
item.description: UK financial firms can adopt tokenisation and distributed ledger technology (DLT) with greater confidence, as the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and the Bank of England set out a shared vision and seek industry views on the future of UK wholesale markets. UK markets have always embraced new technology, and that will be central to ensuring the UK remains at the forefront of global wholesale markets.' Sarah Breeden, deputy governor for financial stability at the Bank of England said: 'The Bank and FCA have done a huge amount to enable the responsible adoption of tokenisation in retail and wholesale finance in the UK, working with the government and the industry. We want to support firms in adopting this technology to lower costs, reduce risk and unlock new services, and our partnership with the Bank of England will ensure a common approach across all parts of wholesale markets.

item.title: TWIA secures $750m of reinsurance from Alamo Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: Before going into the final details on these Alamo Re Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond notes, here’s what we now know about TWIA’s reinsurance tower for 2026 so far. (Series 2026-1) catastrophe bond sponsorship, securing the upper-end target for $750 million of capital markets backed reinsurance limit from this issuance, Artemis can report. The Texas Windstorm Insurance Association (TWIA), the residual market property insurer for the State of Texas, returned to the cat bond market in April with an initial target to secure $450 million of limit from this Alamo Re 2026-1 issuance .

item.title: Nationwide Mutual secures $350m of reinsurance from Aquila Re I 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: Nationwide Mutual ventured back to the catastrophe bond market in April, with an initial target to secure $200 million of multi-peril and multi-year indemnity triggered and per-occurrence based reinsurance protection from this Aquila Re I 2026-1 cat bond transaction. Now, we’re told that Nationwide Mutual has priced the notes to successfully secure the upsized $350 million of reinsurance from this new Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 catastrophe bond, while securing the coverage at the low-ends of reduced price guidance. With the two tranches of Aquila Re I Series 2026-1 cat bond notes now priced, we know that this new cat bond sponsorship with provide Nationwide Mutual with $350 million of protection against losses from the perils of US named storm, earthquake, severe thunderstorm, winter storm, wildfire, meteorite impact, volcanic eruption.

item.title: Jamaica now targets upsized $200m of parametric insurance from new catastrophe bond
item.description: The Government of Jamaica has raised the target size for its third catastrophe bond, now aiming to secure $200 million of parametric named storm and hurricane insurance protection from the World Bank facilitated IBRD CAR Jamaica 2026 issuance, market sources have told Artemis. Jamaica returned for its third catastrophe bond sponsorship at the end of April, initially targeting $150 million of parametric named storm and hurricane protection . Jamaica first secured parametric hurricane insurance disaster risk protection from the capital markets through the $185 million IBRD CAR 130 cat bond in 2021.
